rulings.Case Title: RODRICK BRECKLER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 9, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES U.S.A., INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE STAY APPLIES TO THE COURT RULING ON THE MOTION. IT DOES NOT STAY THE NEED TO FILE A RESPONSE TO THE MOTION. MORIN V. ROSENTHAL (2004) 122 CAL.APP.4TH 673, 680. TOYOTA WAS NAMED AS A DOE DEFENANT WITHOUT COMPLYING WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1324(b); WALLIS V. SOUTHERN PAC. TRANSP. CO. (1976) 61 CAL.APP.3D 782, 786. =(302/PJM/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEON ALLEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BHC)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 9, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION, PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OWNER/PREMISES LIABILITY AND PRODUCTS LIABILITY. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT FOR NEITHER THE TRIAL TESTIMONY OF CALVILLO NOR CANTLEY IS ADMISSIBLE. PLANTS REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE TO DEPOSE BOREM IS GRANTED AND THE HEARING ON THE REMAINING ISSUES IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 2, 2009 AT 9:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. FURTHER PLEADINGS TO BE SUBMITTED BY JANUARY 29, 2009. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MILTON SIEFKEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 9, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION, OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY, AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE WHEN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ARE CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED NO BASIS FOR THE COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT PLAINTIFF'S RECOLLECTION WAS REFRESHED AFTER HIS DEPOSITION. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 22, 2009 FOR DEFENDANT TO BE GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO DEPOSE MR. MUHILLY, GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF PREVIOUSLY DECLINED TO PRODUCE HIM FOR A DEPOSITION. CALVILLO'S TESTIMONY IN A PRIOR CASE IS NOT ADMISSIBLE IN CONNECTION WITH THIS MOTION. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JOILENE GROVE VS. AL CABRINI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 9, 2009, line 11, PLAINTIFFS JOILENE GROVE AND DAVID GROVES'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE MOTION IS DENIED. AS TO ALL 4 ISSUES, PLAINTIFF PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT, IN TURN, HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE ON EACH ISSUE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/JW)
